expectations.text: Uses TaskEither (TE.TaskEither or TE.tryCatch) for async operations
expectations.evidence: Lines 65, 80, 95: fetchUserTE, fetchCartTE, chargePaymentMethodTE all use TE.tryCatch

expectations.text: Uses pipe() to compose the steps sequentially
expectations.evidence: Line 126: checkout function uses pipe(TE.Do, TE.bind(...), ...) throughout

expectations.text: Uses TE.chain or Do notation (TE.bind) to sequence dependent steps
expectations.evidence: Lines 131-149: TE.Do + TE.bind used for user, cart, payment steps

expectations.text: Uses TE.filterOrElse or similar to validate the non-empty cart
Defines a typed error type (discriminated union or similar) rather than generic Error
expectations.evidence: Lines 44-50: CheckoutError discriminated union with readonly code tags

expectations.text: Uses TE.fold or similar to handle the final result
expectations.evidence: Lines 166-189: TE.fold at the HTTP handler edge with exhaustive switch on err.code
